title: Bisect a failure to its first cause
description: Walk commits or the log to find the earliest change or first failing step that introduced the failure, not the last error printed.
body: Steps: 1. Confirm the failure reproduces reliably. 2. In the log, scroll to the FIRST failing command/step, since later errors are often cascades. 3. If a code change is suspected, bisect commits between last-known-good and first-bad. 4. Identify the single commit or step that introduced it. 5. Record the first cause and its evidence. Rollback point: before applying a fix to the suspected commit. Validation: reverting or fixing the identified first cause makes the pipeline green, and downstream errors disappear; fixing the last error in the log without finding the first cause usually treats a symptom and the failure returns.
